wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the project overview section focused on Clawra: 动力学本体认知引擎 (Agent Growth SDK). Explain its purpose as a neuro-symbolic cognitive agent framework designed for industrial and complex business scenarios. Document the core philosophy of bridging large language models with symbolic logic through ontology constraints, deterministic rule engines, and GraphRAG. Include the project's breakthrough approach to achieving logical consistency and zero hallucination characteristics. Provide both conceptual overviews for beginners unfamiliar with neuro-symbolic systems and technical details for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase including terms like '动力学本体' (dynamic ontology), '认知中枢' (cognitive orchestrator), and 'GraphRAG'. Include practical examples demonstrating the framework's unique value proposition over traditional LLM approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: Hierarchical Document Chunking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the hierarchical document chunking subsystem. Explain the multi-level chunking algorithm that intelligently segments long documents based on structural elements like headings, tables, and paragraphs. Document the HierarchicalChunker class implementation, including token-based chunk size management, overlap strategies, and fallback mechanisms. Detail the chunking hierarchy from major sections to fine-grained paragraphs, explaining how the system preserves semantic coherence while maintaining optimal chunk sizes for LLM processing. Include configuration parameters like max_tokens and overlap_tokens, and demonstrate with concrete examples how different document types (technical manuals, reports, contracts) are processed. Address edge cases like malformed documents, very short texts, and mixed content formats.

wiki_catalogs.name: Layered Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the layered architecture design of Clawra. Explain the four-layer system: Perception (Extraction), Cognition (Reasoning), Memory (Storage), and Execution (Action). Document the responsibilities of each layer and how they interact sequentially. Detail the Orchestrator as the cognitive central nervous system coordinating between layers. Explain the data transformation pipeline from raw text through extraction, reasoning, memory storage, and execution. Include architectural diagrams showing layer boundaries and data flow directions. Address the design rationale behind this layered approach and how it enables modularity and maintainability.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Inference Algorithms
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the core inference algorithms section. Document the forward chaining and backward chaining mechanisms with their implementation details, algorithmic complexity, and performance characteristics. Explain the pattern matching system for variable substitution, rule application logic, and fact derivation processes. Include mathematical foundations of rule-based inference, variable unification algorithms, and constraint satisfaction techniques. Provide concrete examples of rule patterns, fact matching scenarios, and inference step-by-step execution. Document the circuit breaker mechanisms for preventing infinite loops and managing computational resources. Address optimization strategies for large-scale knowledge bases and memory management during inference operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Agent Architecture and Base Classes
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the agent architecture foundation. Explain the BaseAgent abstract class design, its role as the foundation for all agent types, and the common interface patterns. Document the abstract method structure, initialization parameters, and the relationship with the Reasoner core. Include architectural patterns used in the agent system, inheritance hierarchies, and extension points. Provide guidelines for implementing custom agent types and maintaining consistency with the base architecture. Address the design decisions behind the abstract base class approach and its benefits for agent extensibility.

wiki_catalogs.name: Semantic Memory
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the semantic memory component focused on hybrid knowledge storage architecture. Document the Neo4j graph database integration for structured knowledge representation and ChromaDB vector store for semantic similarity search. Explain the dual-layer storage mechanism that combines precise graph relationships with fuzzy semantic matching. Detail entity normalization processes including synonym mapping, exact matching, and containment-based resolution. Document the grain cardinality enforcement system for entity relationship constraints. Include practical examples of fact storage, graph traversal queries, and semantic similarity searches. Address connection management, mock mode operations, and performance optimization strategies for large-scale knowledge bases.

wiki_catalogs.name: Confidence Calculation Algorithms
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for confidence calculation algorithms. Document the four primary calculation methods: weighted averaging, Bayesian inference, multiplicative synthesis, and Dempster-Shafer evidence theory. Explain the mathematical foundations, assumptions, and use cases for each method. Include detailed examples of evidence weighting, likelihood ratio calculations, and belief function computations. Document the ConfidenceCalculator class interface, parameter configurations, and source reliability weighting systems. Provide practical examples showing how different methods handle conflicting evidence and produce varying confidence scores. Address the trade-offs between computational complexity and accuracy for each approach.

wiki_catalogs.name: REST API Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive REST API documentation for the Clawra Cognitive Engine. Document all HTTP endpoints including health checks, knowledge management, query operations, reasoning functions, rule management, and interactive interfaces. For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas using Pydantic models, authentication requirements, error codes, and practical usage examples. Include detailed parameter descriptions, validation rules, and response formats. Document the unified interaction endpoint, knowledge ingestion workflow, semantic querying capabilities, and reasoning engine operations. Provide authentication mechanisms, rate limiting considerations, and security headers. Include curl examples and common integration patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Contradiction Detection and Prevention
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the contradiction detection system focused on preventing knowledge contamination and logical inconsistencies. Document the ContradictionChecker class implementation including antonym discovery through OWL disjointWith relationships and fallback mechanisms. Explain the fact validation process, predicate-object pair conflict resolution, and the integration with Neo4j for semantic disconnection detection. Include practical examples of conflict scenarios, threshold configurations, and the decision-making logic for safe knowledge insertion. Address the mathematical foundations of logical consistency and the relationship between confidence levels and conflict severity. Document the logging and error handling mechanisms for failed validations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Health and Status Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the health and status monitoring endpoints. Document the root endpoint (/) that returns API information, health check endpoint (/health) for system status verification, and status endpoint (/status) for comprehensive system metrics. Include detailed response schemas with KnowledgeStatus model fields, timestamp formatting, service availability indicators, and uptime calculations. Provide curl examples for each endpoint, error handling scenarios, and integration patterns for monitoring systems. Document the API key verification mechanism and security considerations for production deployments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Basic Tutorials
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for basic tutorials section. Document the fundamental examples including hello_ontology.py for absolute beginners and complete_integration_demo.py for basic system integration. Explain step-by-step how to set up the environment, configure dependencies, and run simple ontology operations. Include detailed code walkthroughs explaining each component's role in the knowledge processing pipeline. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common setup issues and environment configuration problems. Address different skill levels from complete beginners to developers with some experience in knowledge graphs.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content focused on rapid onboarding to the Clawra framework. Document installation prerequisites including Python 3.10+, Neo4j setup, and dependency management. Provide step-by-step setup instructions with environment configuration examples. Include the quick start tutorial using hello_ontology.py to demonstrate basic framework usage. Document the Streamlit console application for visualizing reasoning processes. Explain the .env configuration file structure with API key setup for Volcengine Ark (Doubao Pro). Include troubleshooting common setup issues and verification steps to ensure proper installation. Make content accessible to developers new to neuro-symbolic systems while providing clear next steps for deeper exploration.

wiki_catalogs.name: LLM Integration and Fact Extraction
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the LLM integration and fact extraction pipeline. Thoroughly explain the KnowledgeExtractor class architecture, including the domain ontology schema definition, system/user prompts, and structured output formatting. Document the Pydantic FactSchema model and KnowledgeExtractionResult structure. Detail the LLM client initialization, API configuration, and retry mechanisms for handling rate limits and failures. Explain the JSON repair capabilities that handle truncated or malformed LLM outputs. Include concrete examples of extraction prompts for different domains, confidence scoring mechanisms, and the transformation from extracted facts to core Fact objects. Address mock LLM mode for testing, environment variable configuration, and troubleshooting common extraction issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Interactions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for component interaction patterns within the Clawra architecture. Document the communication protocols between KnowledgeExtractor, Reasoner, Memory systems, and Agents. Explain the message passing patterns, event-driven interactions, and synchronous/asynchronous communication flows. Detail the role of the Orchestrator in mediating component interactions and managing workflow coordination. Include sequence diagrams showing typical interaction scenarios. Address error propagation, failure handling, and circuit breaker patterns used in component communication. Document the adapter pattern implementation for decoupling components and enabling pluggable integrations.

wiki_catalogs.name: OWL Compliance and Semantics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for OWL compliance and semantic web standards. Document the open world assumption (OWA) implementation, semantic web vocabulary usage with RDFLib, and OWL reasoning capabilities. Explain the distinction between OWL constraints and traditional database constraints, the handling of owl:someValuesFrom and owl:allValuesFrom constructs, and cardinality restrictions. Detail the semantic interpretation of rules, property assertions, and class hierarchies. Include practical examples of OWL-compliant reasoning patterns, semantic validation processes, and integration with external ontologies. Address common pitfalls in OWL reasoning and best practices for maintaining semantic consistency.

wiki_catalogs.name: Orchestrator Agent
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Orchestrator Agent, the cognitive central nervous system of the metacognitive framework. Thoroughly explain its role as the primary coordinator for complex reasoning tasks, decision tracing capabilities, and audit trail management. Document the orchestrator's advanced reasoning coordination, task decomposition strategies, and multi-agent workflow orchestration. Include concrete examples of orchestrator behavior in complex scenarios, decision-making processes, and conflict resolution mechanisms. Explain the integration with the reasoning engine for quality assessment and the memory system for experience tracking. Address the orchestrator's self-monitoring capabilities and performance optimization strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Episodic Memory
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the episodic memory system focused on trajectory recording and learning from experience. Document the SQLite-based persistence layer for storing agent experiences, decision traces, and reasoning processes. Explain the episode data structure including task_id, timestamps, episode_data JSON, reward scores, and human corrections. Detail the trajectory analysis capabilities for identifying success patterns and failure recovery mechanisms. Document the reinforcement learning feedback integration (RLHF) system for human-in-the-loop learning. Include practical examples of episode storage, retrieval, human feedback incorporation, and trajectory pattern recognition. Address database schema design, data lifecycle management, and future reinforcement learning applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Confidence Propagation and Chain Reasoning
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for confidence propagation mechanisms through logical reasoning chains. Explain how confidence values are transmitted and modified as they flow through inference steps. Document the four propagation methods: minimum value, arithmetic mean, geometric mean, and multiplicative combination. Include mathematical formulations and step-by-step examples of confidence decay through complex reasoning chains. Explain the relationship between premise confidence, rule strength, and conclusion reliability. Address how contradictory evidence affects confidence propagation and the strategies for handling inconsistent logical chains. Provide guidelines for setting confidence thresholds and determining when reasoning chains become unreliable.

wiki_catalogs.name: GraphQL Schema
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create GraphQL API documentation for the knowledge graph interface. Document the GraphQL schema definitions, query patterns, mutation operations, and subscription handling. Include type definitions for facts, rules, reasoning results, and confidence levels. Provide query examples for knowledge exploration, rule evaluation, and reasoning traces. Document mutation operations for adding facts and rules, and subscription patterns for real-time updates. Include schema introspection capabilities, error handling patterns, and performance optimization strategies for complex queries.

wiki_catalogs.name: Rule Engine and Mathematical Sandbox
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the rule engine and mathematical sandbox system focused on AST-level safety enforcement. Document the rule compilation process, syntax validation, and execution environment isolation. Explain the sandboxing mechanisms that prevent unsafe operations and mathematical constraint enforcement. Include detailed examples of rule definition syntax, constraint validation, and execution flow control. Document the integration with the orchestrator for decision auditing and the relationship between rule complexity and execution safety. Address the mathematical foundations of constraint satisfaction and the performance implications of sandbox overhead. Provide troubleshooting guidance for rule compilation errors and execution failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Knowledge Management Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for knowledge management operations including text ingestion (/knowledge/ingest), direct fact addition (/knowledge/facts), fact listing (/knowledge/facts), and knowledge clearing (/knowledge/facts/clear). Document the UserInput and FactInput request models, response schemas using ClawraResponse, and error handling patterns. Include practical examples of knowledge ingestion workflows, fact validation rules, confidence scoring mechanisms, and filtering options. Provide authentication requirements, rate limiting considerations, and best practices for bulk knowledge operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Intermediate Demonstrations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for intermediate demonstration section. Cover confidence reasoning examples, supplier monitoring scenarios, and the comprehensive hiring assistant demo. Explain how to implement confidence-aware reasoning, handle real-world business scenarios like supplier risk assessment, and build complete applications with multiple integrated components. Include detailed explanations of the hiring assistant's resume parsing, candidate matching, and interview question generation workflows. Provide guidance on adapting these patterns for other domains and integrating with external systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the core system design of Clawra. Describe the layered architecture pattern with Perception, Cognition, Memory, and Execution layers. Document component interactions, data flows, and integration patterns between the four main layers. Explain the modular design principles, high cohesion and low coupling architecture. Include system context diagrams showing how KnowledgeExtractor, Reasoner, Memory systems, and Agents interact. Address the hybrid memory architecture combining Neo4j graph storage with ChromaDB vector storage. Document the role of the Orchestrator as the cognitive central nervous system. Explain technical decisions behind the neuro-symbolic integration approach and architectural trade-offs. Include infrastructure requirements, scalability considerations, and deployment topology.

wiki_catalogs.name: JSON Repair and Validation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the JSON repair and validation system. Explain the _repair_json method's multi-strategy approach to fixing malformed LLM outputs, including markdown code fence removal, bracket matching, and truncation recovery. Document each repair strategy with examples of before/after transformations. Detail the validation logic that ensures extracted facts meet schema requirements, including confidence score ranges, empty value filtering, and duplicate detection. Include troubleshooting guidance for common JSON parsing failures, malformed outputs, and edge cases in LLM responses. Provide examples of repair scenarios and demonstrate the robustness of the validation pipeline.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for data flow patterns throughout the Clawra system. Document the primary data pathways from text ingestion through extraction, reasoning, memory storage, and retrieval. Explain the transformation of data types at each stage: raw text → structured facts → reasoned conclusions → stored knowledge. Detail the confidence propagation mechanism and how uncertainty flows through the system. Include data flow diagrams showing the lifecycle of information from ingestion to final decision-making. Address batch processing vs streaming data flows, caching strategies, and data consistency guarantees across the system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Confidence Propagation Mechanics

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content for uncertainty quantification and quality assessment systems. wiki_catalogs.name: Query and Reasoning Endpoints

Document performance profiling techniques, resource utilization patterns, and bottleneck identification strategies. Explain optimization strategies for large-scale knowledge bases, including indexing optimizations, query performance tuning, and memory management. Include guidelines for optimizing reasoning performance, confidence calculation efficiency, and memory access patterns. Document monitoring and observability practices, performance metrics collection, and alerting strategies. Address horizontal and vertical scaling considerations, load balancing approaches, and distributed deployment patterns. Provide benchmarking methodologies, performance regression testing, and capacity planning guidelines for production environments.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Clawra: Cognitive Logical Agent with Reasoning & Autonomy

## Project Introduction

Clawra is an advanced neuro-symbolic cognitive platform designed to bridge the gap between natural language understanding and formal logical reasoning. Built around the principle that "ontologies are instructions and rules are laws," the platform transforms unstructured text into high-dimensional ontological tensors and executes deterministic logical closures through sophisticated reasoning engines.

### Purpose Statement
Clawra serves as a comprehensive knowledge processing system that combines symbolic reasoning with neural language models to create autonomous cognitive agents capable of handling complex industrial domains like gas regulation systems, procurement compliance, and technical documentation analysis.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Knowledge Integration**: Seamlessly integrate structured and unstructured knowledge from diverse sources
- **Autonomous Reasoning**: Enable machines to perform logical deduction with confidence assessment
- **Industrial Application**: Specialize in technical domains requiring precise regulatory compliance
- **Cognitive Evolution**: Support continuous learning through metacognitive reflection and self-correction

### Target Audience
- Industrial engineers and technicians dealing with complex technical documentation
- Compliance officers requiring automated regulatory validation
- AI researchers exploring neuro-symbolic integration
- Enterprise developers building intelligent knowledge systems

## Technical Architecture

The Clawra platform follows a layered architecture that separates concerns between perception, reasoning, memory, and execution layers.

```mermaid
graph TD
    subgraph "Perception Layer"
        A[KnowledgeExtractor] --> B[HierarchicalChunker]
        A --> C[LLM Integration]
    end
    
    subgraph "Cognitive Layer"
        D[MetacognitiveAgent] --> E[Reasoner]
        D --> F[CognitiveOrchestrator]
    end
    
    subgraph "Memory Layer"
        G[SemanticMemory] --> H[Neo4j GraphDB]
        G --> I[Chroma Vector Store]
        J[EpisodicMemory] --> K[SQLite Storage]
    end
    
    subgraph "Execution Layer"
        L[RuleEngine] --> M[SafeMathSandbox]
        N[ConfidenceCalculator] --> O[Evidence Propagation]
    end
    
    A --> E
    E --> G
    F --> D
    G --> L
```

### Component Breakdown

**Perception & Extraction**
- KnowledgeExtractor: LLM-powered triple extraction with JSON repair capabilities
- HierarchicalChunker: Advanced text segmentation for large documents
- GlossaryEngine: Domain-specific terminology normalization

**Cognitive Processing**
- MetacognitiveAgent: Self-aware reasoning with confidence calibration
- Reasoner: OWL-compliant logical inference engine
- CognitiveOrchestrator: Task decomposition and tool orchestration

**Memory Management**
- SemanticMemory: Hybrid graph-vector storage with entity normalization
- EpisodicMemory: Persistent experience tracking for reinforcement learning
- VectorAdapter: ChromaDB integration for semantic similarity search

**Rule & Safety Systems**
- RuleEngine: AST-based mathematical validation with sandboxing
- ConfidenceCalculator: Multi-source evidence aggregation
- SafetyMechanisms: Preventive measures against hallucinations

### Design Patterns

The architecture implements several key design patterns:

**Layered Architecture**: Clear separation between perception, reasoning, memory, and execution layers
**Adapter Pattern**: Unified interfaces for different database backends
**Strategy Pattern**: Pluggable extraction strategies for different document types
**Observer Pattern**: Event-driven memory updates and synchronization
**Command Pattern**: Structured tool invocation through orchestrator agents

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Core Platform Interface** (`src/__init__.py`)
The main package exports provide access to all core functionality including reasoning engines, memory systems, and cognitive agents.

**Application Entry Point** (`examples/hello_ontology.py`)
Demonstrates basic usage patterns and provides a foundation for understanding the platform's capabilities.

### Core Modules

**Reasoning Engine** (`src/core/reasoner.py`)
Implements a sophisticated OWL-compliant inference engine supporting both forward and backward chaining with confidence propagation. The engine handles complex logical operations including transitivity, symmetry, and equivalence reasoning.

**Metacognitive Agent** (`src/agents/metacognition.py`)
Features self-reflection capabilities with confidence calibration, knowledge boundary detection, and logical consistency validation. The agent can assess its own reasoning quality and identify potential contradictions.

**Memory Management** (`src/memory/base.py`)
Provides hybrid storage architecture combining Neo4j for structured graph data and ChromaDB for semantic vector search. Includes entity normalization and grain cardinality enforcement.

**Knowledge Extraction** (`src/perception/extractor.py`)
Advanced LLM integration for extracting structured facts from unstructured text with automatic JSON repair and duplicate elimination.

### Configuration Approach

**Project Configuration** (`pyproject.toml`)
Defines build system, dependencies, and development tools. Supports optional Neo4j integration and development dependencies.

**Container Orchestration** (`docker-compose.yml`)
Comprehensive container setup including API service, local LLM (Ollama), GraphDB knowledge graph, and Redis caching layer.

**Build Configuration** (`Dockerfile`)
Multi-stage Docker build process with system dependencies for RDF libraries and optimized runtime environment.

### External Dependencies

**Core Dependencies**:
- `rdflib>=7.0.0`: RDF library for semantic web standards
- `fastapi>=0.109.0`: High-performance web framework
- `neo4j>=5.0.0`: Graph database integration
- `chromadb>=0.4.0`: Vector database for semantic search

**Development Dependencies**:
- `pytest>=8.0.0`: Testing framework
- `ruff>=0.1.0`: Code quality and linting
- `mypy>=1.8.0`: Static type checking

### Integration Points

**LLM Integration** (`src/perception/extractor.py`)
Seamless integration with multiple LLM providers through configurable API clients and fallback mechanisms.

**Database Abstraction** (`src/memory/vector_adapter.py`, `src/memory/neo4j_adapter.py`)
Unified interface for different storage backends with automatic failover and performance optimization.

**API Layer** (`src/llm/api/main.py`)
RESTful API exposing all platform capabilities with proper authentication and rate limiting.

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class KnowledgeExtractor {
        +extract_from_text(text)
        +_extract_chunk(text)
        +_repair_json(raw)
    }
    
    class Reasoner {
        +forward_chain()
        +backward_chain()
        +add_rule()
        +add_fact()
    }
    
    class MetacognitiveAgent {
        +reflect()
        +check_knowledge_boundary()
        +calibrate_confidence()
    }
    
    class SemanticMemory {
        +store_fact()
        +semantic_search()
        +normalize_entity()
    }
    
    class EpisodicMemory {
        +store_episode()
        +add_human_feedback()
        +analyze_trajectories()
    }
    
    KnowledgeExtractor --> Reasoner : processes facts
    MetacognitiveAgent --> Reasoner : uses for inference
    SemanticMemory --> Reasoner : stores facts
    EpisodicMemory --> MetacognitiveAgent : records experiences
    Reasoner --> SemanticMemory : queries knowledge
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Neuro-Symbolic Integration**
The platform uniquely combines neural language understanding with symbolic reasoning, enabling both creative interpretation and logical precision. This dual approach allows for handling ambiguous natural language while maintaining formal correctness in domain-specific applications.

**Confidence-Aware Reasoning**
Advanced confidence calculation system tracks evidence sources, propagates uncertainty through logical chains, and provides transparent explanations for conclusions. This feature is crucial for industrial applications where decision-making requires quantified uncertainty assessment.

**Hybrid Knowledge Storage**
Multi-modal memory architecture combining graph databases for structured relationships with vector stores for semantic similarity. This hybrid approach enables both precise logical queries and flexible contextual searches.

**Metacognitive Self-Assessment**
Agents can evaluate their own reasoning quality, detect potential contradictions, and identify knowledge boundaries. This self-awareness prevents overconfidence in uncertain situations and triggers appropriate fallback behaviors.

### Implementation Highlights

**Advanced Text Processing** (`src/chunking/hierarchical.py`)
Sophisticated document segmentation that intelligently handles technical documents with complex structures, preserving semantic coherence across chunk boundaries.

**Robust LLM Integration** (`src/perception/extractor.py`)
Production-ready LLM interaction with automatic retry logic, JSON repair capabilities, and fallback mechanisms for reliability in enterprise environments.

**Safety-Critical Rule Execution** (`src/core/ontology/rule_engine.py`)
AST-based mathematical validation with sandboxing prevents dangerous computations and ensures predictable behavior in safety-critical applications.

**Continuous Learning Pipeline** (`src/evolution/self_correction.py`)
Automated reflection and correction mechanisms enable systems to learn from mistakes and improve reasoning accuracy over time.

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Perception
    Perception --> Extraction
    Extraction --> Reasoning
    Reasoning --> MemoryStorage
    MemoryStorage --> Metacognition
    Metacognition --> DecisionMaking
    DecisionMaking --> ActionExecution
    ActionExecution --> FeedbackLoop
    FeedbackLoop --> Perception
    
    state Perception {
        [*] --> TextInput
        TextInput --> Chunking
        Chunking --> LLMProcessing
        LLMProcessing --> StructuredOutput
    }
    
    state Reasoning {
        [*] --> ForwardChain
        ForwardChain --> BackwardChain
        BackwardChain --> ConfidenceAssessment
        ConfidenceAssessment --> Conclusion
    }
    
    state Metacognition {
        [*] --> SelfReflection
        SelfReflection --> BoundaryAssessment
        BoundaryAssessment --> Correction
    }
```

The Clawra platform represents a significant advancement in knowledge processing systems, offering a robust foundation for building intelligent agents capable of handling complex technical domains with both creativity and precision. Its modular architecture supports various deployment scenarios while maintaining the flexibility needed for continuous evolution and improvement.

**Sources:**
- [pyproject.toml](pyproject.toml)
- [src/__init__.py](src/__init__.py)
- [src/core/reasoner.py](src/core/reasoner.py)
- [src/agents/metacognition.py](src/agents/metacognition.py)
- [src/memory/base.py](src/memory/base.py)
- [src/perception/extractor.py](src/perception/extractor.py)
- [examples/hello_ontology.py](examples/hello_ontology.py)
- [Dockerfile](Dockerfile)
- [docker-compose.yml](docker-compose.yml)
- [docs/architecture.md](docs/architecture.md)

</blog>
